data.title: T1 Energy says U.S.tariffs align with its strategy
data.description:
T1 Energy (NYSE:TE) said that the tariff imposition that rocked the markets this week align with the company’s strategy to establish a vertically integrated U.S. solar-plus-storage supply chain.

With T1’s operating 5 GW solar module facility, G1 Dallas, ramping up production, and project development activities underway for the planned G2 Austin U.S. solar cell facility, T1 (NYSE:TE) is building its American manufacturing footprint and bringing new jobs to Texas.

“The United States needs more domestically produced energy in all forms, and we are positioning T1 to lead a domestic solar manufacturing revival built on leading edge technologies and job creation.” said Daniel Barcelo, Chairman and CEO.

The stock price dropped 1.82% on Thursday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: Banco Santander expects Q1 RoTE post-AT1 of 15.7%
data.description:
• Banco Santander (NYSE:SAN) announced on Friday that it expects to achieve a 15.7% RoTE post-AT1 in the first quarter.
  • The bank said it expects to increase Q1 profitability relative to 2024; grow tangible net asset value plus cash dividend per share of over 14%; while maintaining solid capital generation, with a CET1 ratio of 12.9% (up 10 basis points against FY 2024).
  • The Spanish lender maintained its 2025 target of around €62B in revenue; falling costs year-on-year in absolute terms; a stable cost of risk – with better performing markets offsetting others; and reaching a RoTE of 16.5% post-AT1 and a CET1 of 13%. 
  • Additionally, the Santander board approved a final cash dividend of 11 euro cents per share, payable from May 2.

data.title: Hershey is expected to see upside from its strategic deal for LesserEvil
data.description:
Hershey (NYSE:HSY) acquired LesserEvil this week in a deal that is seen complementing its growing salty snack portfolio. Based in Danbury, Connecticut, LesserEvil is known for producing organic and health-conscious snacks, including popcorn and extruded snacks

LesserEvil was founded in 2004 in Tuckahoe, New York, by Michael Sands, actor Gene Hackman, and financial analyst Jim Cramer. The company initially focused on kettle corn and later expanded its product line to include Krinkle Sticks and other snacks aimed at competing with traditional cheese puffs. LesserEvil achieved profitability in 2021 and continued its upward trajectory, with gross sales surpassing $100 million by 2023. The company now distributes its products across 35,000 retail locations, including at Walmart (WMT) and Costco (COST) locations.

Morgan Stanley analyst Megan Alexander Clapp noted that the acquisition follows the company's Dot's (2021) and Skinny Pop/Pirates Booty (2018) acquisitions in the salty snacks categories. Clapp said the deal expands Hershey's presence in the better-for-you snacking categories, underscoring its desires to enhance topline growth through portfolio diversification. The opportunity for synergies was highlighted.

Clapp estimated that the LesserEvil brand would represent ~1% of Hershey's pro forma revenue in 2026 and contribute about 10-20 basis points to topline growth annually. "We see a sizable runway for continued growth, with its ACV still less than 60%. We also believe there is an opportunity for HSY to realize post-transaction synergies given an ability to leverage LesserEvil's owned manufacturing capabilities within its current salty snacks portfolio, which is ~50% co-manufacturing today," wrote Clapp.

Morgan Stanley has as Equal-weight rating on Hershey (NYSE:HSY) and a price target of $183.

Shares of Hershey inched 0.1% higher in premarket trading on Friday after a 1.8% gain on Thursday amid defensive positioning by investors.

data.title: China strikes back against U.S. with 34% tariffs on all goods
data.description:
Two days after U.S. President Donald Trump announced sweeping tariffs against several countries, China has retaliated with 34% tariffs on all US imports from April 10, reported by the country’s official Xinhua News Agency.

Chinese officials announced a series of new trade measures, including an immediate restriction on the export of seven categories of rare earth materials. They also launched an anti-dumping investigation into CT X-ray tubes imported from the United States and India, and suspended poultry imports from two U.S. companies. In addition, 11 American defense firms have been added to China’s unreliable entities list, while export controls are being placed on 16 other U.S. companies.

The 34% levy imposed by China matches the “reciprocal” tariff announced by Trump on Wednesday, which itself added to the existing tariffs on the country. Once the latest U.S. tariffs come into effect on April 9, Chinese goods will face a 54% tax rate, which China’s finance ministry said was “inconsistent with international trade rules.”

The ministry has called for the U.S. to withdraw its tariffs and negotiate trade differences through mutual consultation.

U.S. stock futures fell to session lows on Friday after China’s retaliatory levies. Dow-linked futures (INDU) are down just over -2%, while S&P futures (SPX) are -2.6%. Nasdaq 100 Futures (US100:IND) are also down -2.7%.

Major U.S. stock market indices have already taken a beating in the days since Trump’s announcement. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was down nearly 4% on Thursday while S&P 500 (SP500) was down nearly -5%.

European stocks have also been hit by the news.

China ETFs: (KWEB), (PGJ), (FXI), and (ASHR).

China-exposed tech companies to watch: Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Taiwan Manufacturing Company (TSM), Tesla (TSLA)

data.title: Dr. Mehmet Oz wins Senate confirmation to lead Medicare and Medicaid agency
data.description:
The U.S. Senate has confirmed former heart surgeon and TV personality Dr. Mehmet Oz as the head of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to lead an agency facing massive funding cuts under the Trump administration. 

The confirmation decided on Thursday in a party-line vote of 53-45 will allow Dr. Oz to become the commissioner of CMS under the supervision of Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the new secretary of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.

Dr. Oz, who has served as a distinguished heart surgeon at Columbia University for years, takes over the CMS at a time when the HHS and its agencies are grappling with tens of thousands of job cuts initiated as part of a sweeping restructuring program introduced by RFK Jr.

While the CMS was largely spared from layoffs, Republican lawmakers are currently considering billions of dollars in funding cuts for the agency’s Medicaid program jointly run by the federal government and states.

During a confirmation hearing last month, Dr. Oz refused to oppose the proposed funding cuts, noting that "the way you protect Medicaid is by making sure it is viable on every level."

data.title: Flight to safety: US10Y falls below 4%; eyes on Fed's Powell and payrolls data
data.description:
Markets are still reeling from President Donald Trump's aggressive across-the-board tariffs, which fueled a global stock selloff, wiped out over $2T from Wall Street and reignited concerns of a prolonged trade war.  

Investors rushed to safer assets like bonds, with the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) falling below 4% for the first time since October 2024. At the time of writing, (US10Y) was down 17 bps at 3.88%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 21 bps to 3.52%.

Traders now await Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech on Friday, hoping he'll offer some clues on the U.S. economy and what the tariffs could mean for the central bank's monetary policy.

Also in focus is the March nonfarm payrolls data, which will be released before Powell takes the stage. Economists are expecting a slowdown in jobs growth, and an uptick in unemployment.

Markets are now increasingly expecting the Fed to lower interest rates by at least 100 basis points this year, possibly through four separate 25-bp cuts starting in June. Traders had been factoring in about 75 bps of cuts before the tariffs were announced.

"Fed officials have tried to play it cool recently," said ING's Chris Turner. "Given that the market is now fully pricing a 25-bp June cut, any suggestion that the central bank will not be rushed into rate cuts could hit equities and subsequently weigh on the dollar too."

data.title: Matador Resources sells remaining Eagle Ford assets in deals exceeding $30M
data.description:
Matador Resources (NYSE:MTDR) said Friday it sold its remaining Eagle Ford shale position in south Texas in series of transactions totaling more than $30M over the last two quarters.

Matador (NYSE:MTDR) said it applied a portion of its cash flows and over $30M in proceeds from the sale to reduce debt; in total, the company repaid $180M of borrowings under its credit facility during Q1 and ended the quarter with $405M under the facility, based on a preliminary review.

"Matador is excited to continue its primary focus on developing its high-quality acreage in the northern Delaware Basin," Chairman and CEO Joseph Foran said, adding the company owns ~200K net acres in the basin, 80% held by production.

The company also said it has already secured inventory for the majority of its 2025 drilling program, and does not expect any recent tariffs to impact its well costs until this year's H2.

Matador (MTDR) shares plunged 14.6% on Thursday, falling alongside most other oil and gas stocks in response to President Trump's tariff announcement.

data.title: MarketAxess delivers 31% higher average daily trading volume in Q1
Maritime Resources issues shares for interest payment
data.description:
Maritime Resources (TSXV:MAE:CA) (OTCPK:MRTMF) said that its previously announced contemplated issuance of common shares to holders of $5M non-convertible senior secured notes issued on August 14, 2023.

The company has issued ~2.57M interest shares in satisfaction of the interest payable thereunder as of March 31, 2025 in the aggregate amount of $129,500.

In accordance with the terms of the notes, the company issued the interest shares in satisfaction of the interest payment at a deemed price of C$0.072 per interest share. T
